5	[project]
6	name = "fastapi"

8	description = "FastAPI framework, high performance, easy to learn, fast to code, ready for production"

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)
54	
55

6	# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
7	# in their own AsyncExitStack
8	class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
9	    def __init__(
10	        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
11	    ) -> None:
12	        self.app = app
13	        self.context_name = context_name
14	
15	    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
16	        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
17	            scope[self.context_name] = stack
18	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

68	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1, "subcounter": 1}
69	    response = client.get("/sub-counter/")
70	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
71	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 2, "subcounter": 2}
72	
73	
74	def test_sub_counter_no_cache():
75	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
76	    response = client.get("/sub-counter-no-cache/")
77	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
78	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 2, "subcounter": 1}
79	    response = client.get("/sub-counter-no-cache/")
80	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
81	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 4, "subcounter": 3}
82	
83	
84	def test_security_cache():
85	    counter_holder["counter"] = 0
86	    response = client.get("/scope-counter/")
87	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
88	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 1, "scope_counter_1": 2, "scope_counter_2": 2}
89	    response = client.get("/scope-counter/")
90	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
91	    assert response.json() == {"counter": 3, "scope_counter_1": 4, "scope_counter_2": 4}
92

1	import pytest
2	from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI
3	from fastapi.routing import APIRoute
4	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
5	from inline_snapshot import snapshot
6	from starlette.routing import Route
7	
8	app = FastAPI()
9	
10	
11	class APIRouteA(APIRoute):
12	    x_type = "A"
13	
14	
15	class APIRouteB(APIRoute):
16	    x_type = "B"
17	
18	
19	class APIRouteC(APIRoute):
20	    x_type = "C"
21	
22	
23	router_a = APIRouter(route_class=APIRouteA)
24	router_b = APIRouter(route_class=APIRouteB)
25	router_c = APIRouter(route_class=APIRouteC)
26	
27	
28	@router_a.get("/")
29	def get_a():
30	    return {"msg": "A"}
31	
32	
33	@router_b.get("/")
34	def get_b():
35	    return {"msg": "B"}
36	
37	
38	@router_c.get("/")
39	def get_c():
40	    return {"msg": "C"}
41	
42	
43	router_b.include_router(router=router_c, prefix="/c")
44	router_a.include_router(router=router_b, prefix="/b")
45	app.include_router(router=router_a, prefix="/a")
46	
47	
48	client = TestClient(app)
49	
50	
51	@pytest.mark.parametrize(
52	    "path,expected_status,expected_response",
53	    [
54	        ("/a", 200, {"msg": "A"}),
55	        ("/a/b", 200, {"msg": "B"}),
56	        ("/a/b/c", 200, {"msg": "C"}),
57	    ],
58	)
59	def test_get_path(path, expected_status, expected_response):
60	    response = client.get(path)
61	    assert response.status_code == expected_status
62	    assert response.json() == expected_response
63	
64	
65	def test_route_classes():
66	    routes = {}
67	    for r in app.router.routes:
68	        assert isinstance(r, Route)
69	        routes[r.path] = r
70	    assert getattr(routes["/a/"], "x_type") == "A"  # noqa: B009
71	    assert getattr(routes["/a/b/"], "x_type") == "B"  # noqa: B009
72	    assert getattr(routes["/a/b/c/"], "x_type") == "C"  # noqa: B009
73	
74	
75	def test_openapi_schema():
76	    response = client.get("/openapi.json")
77	    assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
78	    assert response.json() == snapshot(
79	        {
80	            "openapi": "3.1.0",
81	            "info": {"title": "FastAPI", "version": "0.1.0"},
82	            "paths": {
83	                "/a/": {
84	                    "get": {
85	                        "responses": {
86	                            "200": {
87	                                "description": "Successful Response",
88	                                "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {}}},
89	                            }
90	                        },
91	                        "summary": "Get A",
92	                        "operationId": "get_a_a__get",
93	                    }
94	                },
95	                "/a/b/": {
96	                    "get": {
97	                        "responses": {
98	                            "200": {
99	                                "description": "Successful Response",
100	                                "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {}}},
101	                            }
102	                        },
103	                        "summary": "Get B",
104	                        "operationId": "get_b_a_b__get",
105	                    }
106	                },
107	                "/a/b/c/": {
108	                    "get": {
109	                        "responses": {
110	                            "200": {
111	                                "description": "Successful Response",
112	                                "content": {"application/json": {"schema": {}}},
113	                            }
114	                        },
115	                        "summary": "Get C",
116	                        "operationId": "get_c_a_b_c__get",
117	                    }
118	                },
119	            },
120	        }
121	    )

200	            ),
201	        ] = "0.1.0",
202	        openapi_url: Annotated[
203	            str | None,
204	            Doc(
205	                """
206	                The URL where the OpenAPI schema will be served from.
207	
208	                If you set it to `None`, no OpenAPI schema will be served publicly, and
209	                the default automatic endpoints `/docs` and `/redoc` will also be
210	                disabled.
211	
212	                Read more in the
213	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#openapi-url).
214	
215	                **Example**
216	
217	                ```python
218	                from fastapi import FastAPI
219	
220	                app = FastAPI(openapi_url="/api/v1/openapi.json")
221	                ```
222	                """
223	            ),
224	        ] = "/openapi.json",
225	        openapi_tags: Annotated[
226	            list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
227	            Doc(
228	                """
229	                A list of tags used by OpenAPI, these are the same `tags` you can set
230	                in the *path operations*, like:
231	
232	                * `@app.get("/users/", tags=["users"])`
233	                * `@app.get("/items/", tags=["items"])`
234	
235	                The order of the tags can be used to specify the order shown in
236	                tools like Swagger UI, used in the automatic path `/docs`.
237	
238	                It's not required to specify all the tags used.
239	
240	                The tags that are not declared MAY be organized randomly or based
241	                on the tools' logic. Each tag name in the list MUST be unique.
242	
243	                The value of each item is a `dict` containing:
244	
245	                * `name`: The name of the tag.
246	                * `description`: A short description of the tag.
247	                    [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/) MAY be used for rich
248	                    text representation.
249	                * `externalDocs`: Additional external documentation for this tag. If
250	                    provided, it would contain a `dict` with:
251	                    * `description`: A short description of the target documentation.
252	                        [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/) MAY be used for
253	                        rich text representation.
254	                    * `url`: The URL for the target documentation. Value MUST be in
255	                        the form of a URL.
256	
257	                Read more in the
258	                [FastAPI docs for Metadata and Docs URLs](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/metadata/#metadata-for-tags).
259	
260	                **Example**
261	
262	                ```python
263	                from fastapi import FastAPI
264	
265	                tags_metadata = [
266	                    {
267	                        "name": "users",
268	                        "description": "Operations with users. The **login** logic is also here.",
269	                    },
270	                    {
271	                        "name": "items",
272	                        "description": "Manage items. So _fancy_ they have their own docs.",
273	                        "externalDocs": {
274	                            "description": "Items external docs",
275	                            "url": "https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/",
276	                        },
277	                    },
278	                ]
279	
280	                app = FastAPI(openapi_tags=tags_metadata)
281	                ```
282	                """
283	            ),
284	        ] = None,
285	        servers: Annotated[
286	            list[dict[str, str | Any]] | None,
287	            Doc(
288	                """
289	                A `list` of `dict`s with connectivity information to a target server.
290	
291	                You would use it, for example, if your application is served from
292	                different domains and you want to use the same Swagger UI in the
293	                browser to interact with each of them (instead of having multiple
294	                browser tabs open). Or if you want to leave fixed the possible URLs.
295	
296	                If the servers `list` is not provided, or is an empty `list`, the
297	                `servers` property in the generated OpenAPI will be:
298	
299	                * a `dict` with a `url` value of the application's mounting point
300	                (`root_path`) if it's different from `/`.
301	                * otherwise, the `servers` property will be omitted from the OpenAPI
302	                schema.
303	
304	                Each item in the `list` is a `dict` containing:
305	
306	                * `url`: A URL to the target host. This URL supports Server Variables
307	                and MAY be relative, to indicate that the host location is relative
308	                to the location where the OpenAPI document is being served. Variable
309	                substitutions will be made when a variable is named in `{`brackets`}`.
310	                * `description`: An optional string describing the host designated by
311	                the URL. [CommonMark syntax](https://commonmark.org/) MAY be used for
312	                rich text representation.
313	                * `variables`: A `dict` between a variable name and its value. The value
314	                    is used for substitution in the server's URL template.
315	
316	                Read more in the
317	                [FastAPI docs for Behind a Proxy](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/behind-a-proxy/#additional-servers).

324	                app = FastAPI(
325	                    servers=[

3	import pytest
4	from fastapi import APIRouter, FastAPI, Query
5	from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
6	from inline_snapshot import snapshot
7	
8	app = FastAPI()
9	
10	
11	@app.get("/default")
12	async def default(foo: Annotated[str, Query()] = "foo"):
13	    return {"foo": foo}
14	
15	
16	@app.get("/required")
17	async def required(foo: Annotated[str, Query(min_length=1)]):
18	    return {"foo": foo}
19	
20	
21	@app.get("/multiple")
22	async def multiple(foo: Annotated[str, object(), Query(min_length=1)]):
23	    return {"foo": foo}
24	
25	
26	@app.get("/unrelated")
27	async def unrelated(foo: Annotated[str, object()]):
28	    return {"foo": foo}
29	
30	
31	client = TestClient(app)
32	
33	foo_is_missing = {
34	    "detail": [
35	        {
36	            "loc": ["query", "foo"],
37	            "msg": "Field required",
38	            "type": "missing",
39	            "input": None,
40	        }
41	    ]
42	}
43	foo_is_short = {
44	    "detail": [
45	        {
46	            "ctx": {"min_length": 1},
47	            "loc": ["query", "foo"],
48	            "msg": "String should have at least 1 character",
49	            "type": "string_too_short",
50	            "input": "",
51	        }
52	    ]
53	}
54	
55	
56	@pytest.mark.parametrize(
57	    "path,expected_status,expected_response",
58	    [
59	        ("/default", 200, {"foo": "foo"}),
60	        ("/default?foo=bar", 200, {"foo": "bar"}),
61	        ("/required?foo=bar", 200, {"foo": "bar"}),
62	        ("/required", 422, foo_is_missing),
63	        ("/required?foo=", 422, foo_is_short),
64	        ("/multiple?foo=bar", 200, {"foo": "bar"}),
65	        ("/multiple", 422, foo_is_missing),
66	        ("/multiple?foo=", 422, foo_is_short),
67	        ("/unrelated?foo=bar", 200, {"foo": "bar"}),
68	        ("/unrelated", 422, foo_is_missing),
69	    ],
70	)
71	def test_get(path, expected_status, expected_response):
72	    response = client.get(path)
73	    assert response.status_code == expected_status
74	    assert response.json() == expected_response
75	
76	
77	def test_multiple_path():
78	    app = FastAPI()
79	
80	    @app.get("/test1")
81	    @app.get("/test2")
82	    async def test(var: Annotated[str, Query()] = "bar"):
83	        return {"foo": var}
84	
85	    client = TestClient(app)
86	    response = client.get("/test1")
87	    assert response.status_code == 200
88	    assert response.json() == {"foo": "bar"}
89	
90	    response = client.get("/test1", params={"var": "baz"})
91	    assert response.status_code == 200
92	    assert response.json() == {"foo": "baz"}
93	
94	    response = client.get("/test2")
95	    assert response.status_code == 200
96	    assert response.json() == {"foo": "bar"}
97	
98	    response = client.get("/test2", params={"var": "baz"})
99	    assert response.status_code == 200
100	    assert response.json() == {"foo": "baz"}
101	
102	
103	def test_nested_router():
104	    app = FastAPI()
105	
106	    router = APIRouter(prefix="/nested")
107	
108	    @router.get("/test")
109	    async def test(var: Annotated[str, Query()] = "bar"):
110	        return {"foo": var}
111	
112	    app.include_router(router)
113	
114	    client = TestClient(app)
115	
116	    response = client.get("/nested/test")
117	    assert response.status_code == 200
118	    assert response.json() == {"foo": "bar"}

4	from fastapi.websockets import WebSocket
5	from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException
6	from starlette.requests import Request
7	from starlette.responses import JSONResponse, Response
8	from starlette.status import WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION
9	
10	
11	async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException) -> Response:
12	    headers = getattr(exc, "headers", None)
13	    if not is_body_allowed_for_status_code(exc.status_code):
14	        return Response(status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers)
15	    return JSONResponse(
16	        {"detail": exc.detail}, status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers
17	    )
18	
19	
20	async def request_validation_exception_handler(
21	    request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError
22	) -> JSONResponse:
23	    return JSONResponse(
24	        status_code=422,
25	        content={"detail": jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())},
26	    )
27	
28	
29	async def websocket_request_validation_exception_handler(
30	    websocket: WebSocket, exc: WebSocketRequestValidationError
31	) -> None:
32	    await websocket.close(
33	        code=WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION, reason=jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())
34	    )

33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers

75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")

85

72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

41	def test_dependency_contextvars():
42	    """
43	    Check that custom middlewares don't affect the contextvar context for dependencies.
44	
45	    The code before yield and the code after yield should be run in the same contextvar
46	    context, so that request_state_context_var.reset(contextvar_token).
47	
48	    If they are run in a different context, that raises an error.
49	    """

1023	        # Duplicate/override from Starlette to add AsyncExitStackMiddleware

1028	
1029	        for key, value in self.exception_handlers.items():
1030	            if key in (500, Exception):
1031	                error_handler = value
1032	            else:
1033	                exception_handlers[key] = value
1034	
1035	        middleware = (
1036	            [Middleware(ServerErrorMiddleware, handler=error_handler, debug=debug)]  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1037	            + self.user_middleware
1038	            + [
1039	                Middleware(
1040	                    ExceptionMiddleware,  # ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]
1041	                    handlers=exception_handlers,
1042	                    debug=debug,

1045	                # Before this was also used for closing dependencies with yield but

1048	                # versions (as of writing 0.117.1) that allowed doing
1049	                # except HTTPException inside a dependency with yield.
1050	                # This needs to happen after user middlewares because those create a
1051	                # new contextvars context copy by using a new AnyIO task group.

1054	                # intentions.

1057	                # in that internal contextvars context, the values would not be
1058	                # available in the outer context of the AsyncExitStack.

1060	                # user middlewares, the same context is used.
1061	                # This is currently not needed, only for closing files, but used to be
1062	                # important when dependencies with yield were closed here.

1064	            ]

17	class HTTPException(StarletteHTTPException):
18	    """
19	    An HTTP exception you can raise in your own code to show errors to the client.
20	
21	    This is for client errors, invalid authentication, invalid data, etc. Not for server
22	    errors in your code.
23	
24	    Read more about it in the
25	    [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/).
26	
27	    ## Example
28	
29	    ```python
30	    from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException
31	
32	    app = FastAPI()
33	
34	    items = {"foo": "The Foo Wrestlers"}
35	
36	
37	    @app.get("/items/{item_id}")
38	    async def read_item(item_id: str):
39	        if item_id not in items:
40	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Item not found")
41	        return {"item": items[item_id]}
42	    ```
43	    """
44	
45	    def __init__(
46	        self,
47	        status_code: Annotated[
48	            int,
49	            Doc(
50	                """
51	                HTTP status code to send to the client.
52	
53	                Read more about it in the
54	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#use-httpexception)
55	                """
56	            ),
57	        ],
58	        detail: Annotated[
59	            Any,
60	            Doc(
61	                """
62	                Any data to be sent to the client in the `detail` key of the JSON
63	                response.
64	
65	                Read more about it in the
66	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#use-httpexception)
67	                """
68	            ),
69	        ] = None,
70	        headers: Annotated[
71	            Mapping[str, str] | None,
72	            Doc(
73	                """
74	                Any headers to send to the client in the response.
75	
76	                Read more about it in the
77	                [FastAPI docs for Handling Errors](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/handling-errors/#add-custom-headers)
78	
79	                """
80	            ),
81	        ] = None,
82	    ) -> None:
83	        super().__init__(status_code=status_code, detail=detail, headers=headers)
84

86	class WebSocketException(StarletteWebSocketException):
87	    """
88	    A WebSocket exception you can raise in your own code to show errors to the client.
89	
90	    This is for client errors, invalid authentication, invalid data, etc. Not for server
91	    errors in your code.
92	
93	    Read more about it in the
94	    [FastAPI docs for WebSockets](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/websockets/).
95	
96	    ## Example
97	
98	    ```python
99	    from typing import Annotated
100	
101	    from fastapi import (
102	        Cookie,
103	        FastAPI,
104	        WebSocket,
105	        WebSocketException,
106	        status,
107	    )
108	
109	    app = FastAPI()
110	
111	    @app.websocket("/items/{item_id}/ws")
112	    async def websocket_endpoint(
113	        *,
114	        websocket: WebSocket,
115	        session: Annotated[str | None, Cookie()] = None,
116	        item_id: str,
117	    ):
118	        if session is None:
119	            raise WebSocketException(code=status.WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION)
120	        await websocket.accept()
121	        while True:
122	            data = await websocket.receive_text()
123	            await websocket.send_text(f"Session cookie is: {session}")
124	            await websocket.send_text(f"Message text was: {data}, for item ID: {item_id}")
125	    ```
126	    """
127	
128	    def __init__(
129	        self,
130	        code: Annotated[
131	            int,
132	            Doc(
133	                """
134	                A closing code from the
135	                [valid codes defined in the specification](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6455#section-7.4.1).
136	                """
137	            ),
138	        ],
139	        reason: Annotated[
140	            str | None,
141	            Doc(
142	                """
143	                The reason to close the WebSocket connection.
144	
145	                It is UTF-8-encoded data. The interpretation of the reason is up to the
146	                application, it is not specified by the WebSocket specification.
147	
148	                It could contain text that could be human-readable or interpretable
149	                by the client code, etc.
150	                """
151	            ),
152	        ] = None,
153	    ) -> None:
154	        super().__init__(code=code, reason=reason)

174	class ValidationException(Exception):
175	    def __init__(
176	        self,
177	        errors: Sequence[Any],
178	        *,
179	        endpoint_ctx: EndpointContext | None = None,
180	    ) -> None:
181	        self._errors = errors
182	        self.endpoint_ctx = endpoint_ctx
183	
184	        ctx = endpoint_ctx or {}
185	        self.endpoint_function = ctx.get("function")
186	        self.endpoint_path = ctx.get("path")
187	        self.endpoint_file = ctx.get("file")
188	        self.endpoint_line = ctx.get("line")
189	
190	    def errors(self) -> Sequence[Any]:
191	        return self._errors
192	
193	    def _format_endpoint_context(self) -> str:
194	        if not (self.endpoint_file and self.endpoint_line and self.endpoint_function):
195	            if self.endpoint_path:
196	                return f"\n  Endpoint: {self.endpoint_path}"
197	            return ""
198	
199	        context = f'\n  File "{self.endpoint_file}", line {self.endpoint_line}, in {self.endpoint_function}'
200	        if self.endpoint_path:
201	            context += f"\n    {self.endpoint_path}"
202	        return context
203	
204	    def __str__(self) -> str:
205	        message = f"{len(self._errors)} validation error{'s' if len(self._errors) != 1 else ''}:\n"
206	        for err in self._errors:
207	            message += f"  {err}\n"
208	        message += self._format_endpoint_context()
209	        return message.rstrip()
210	
211

219	    ) -> None:
220	        super().__init__(errors, endpoint_ctx=endpoint_ctx)
221	        self.body = body

12	class ContentSizeLimitMiddleware:
13	    """Content size limiting middleware for ASGI applications
14	    Args:
15	      app (ASGI application): ASGI application
16	      max_content_size (optional): the maximum content size allowed in bytes, None for no limit
17	    """
18	
19	    def __init__(self, app: APIRouter, max_content_size: int | None = None):
20	        self.app = app
21	        self.max_content_size = max_content_size
22	
23	    def receive_wrapper(self, receive):
24	        received = 0
25	
26	        async def inner():
27	            nonlocal received
28	            message = await receive()
29	            if message["type"] != "http.request":
30	                return message  # pragma: no cover
31	
32	            body_len = len(message.get("body", b""))
33	            received += body_len
34	            if received > self.max_content_size:
35	                raise HTTPException(
36	                    422,
37	                    detail={
38	                        "name": "ContentSizeLimitExceeded",
39	                        "code": 999,
40	                        "message": "File limit exceeded",
41	                    },
42	                )
43	            return message
44	
45	        return inner
46	
47	    async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send):
48	        if scope["type"] != "http" or self.max_content_size is None:
49	            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
50	            return
51	
52	        wrapper = self.receive_wrapper(receive)
53	        await self.app(scope, wrapper, send)

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the testing setup. Let me create a final summary report:

## COMPREHENSIVE TESTING SETUP ANALYSIS

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here's the complete testing setup:

### 1. **Testing Framework**
- **Framework Used**: **pytest** (version >=9.0.0 per pyproject.toml)
- **Test Client**: **Starlette's TestClient** (FastAPI re-exports it from `fastapi.testclient`)
- **Configuration File**: `pyproject.toml` (pytest configuration in `[tool.pytest]` section)

**Pytest Configuration** (`pyproject.toml` lines 228-239):
```
[tool.pytest]
minversion = "9.0"
addopts = [
  "--strict-config",
  "--strict-markers",
  "--ignore=docs_src",
]
strict_xfail = true
filterwarnings = [
    "error",
]
timeout = "20"
```

### 2. **Test File Structure**
- **Location**: `/tests/` directory at repository root
- **File Pattern**: `test_*.py` files
- **No conftest.py**: There is NO central `conftest.py` configuration file - each test file is self-contained
- **Total Test Files**: 200+ individual test files
- **Special Test Directories**:
  - `tests/benchmarks/` - Performance benchmarks
  - `tests/test_modules_same_name_body/` - Nested test structure with app modules
  - `tests/test_tutorial/` - Tests for documentation examples

### 3. **Test Client Setup Patterns**

**Basic Pattern** (Most Common):
```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI()

# ... define routes ...

client = TestClient(app)

def test_something():
    response = client.get("/path")
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

**Examples from codebase**:
- `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` - Lines 65: `client = TestClient(app)`
- `/tests/test_application.py` - Line 7: `client = TestClient(app)`
- `/tests/test_dependency_cache.py` - Line 51: `client = TestClient(app)`

### 4. **Existing Middleware Test Patterns**

**File**: `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`
- Tests custom ASGI middleware by adding it via `app.add_middleware()`
- Uses `TestClient` context manager: `with client:` (lines 73, 90)
- Tests both success and error cases
- Middleware classes implement `async __call__(self, scope, receive, send)`

**File**: `/tests/test_dependency_contextvars.py` (Lines 22-28)
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def custom_middleware(
    request: Request, call_next: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]]
):
    response = await call_next(request)
    response.headers["custom"] = "foo"
    return response
```

**File**: `/tests/test_dependency_contextmanager.py` (Lines 206-210)
```python
@app.middleware("http")
async def middleware(request, call_next):
    response: StreamingResponse = await call_next(request)
    response.headers["x-state"] = json.dumps(state.copy())
    return response
```

**File**: `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` (Lines 8-30) - Fixture-based approach
```python
@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )
    # ... define routes ...
    return app

@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)
```

### 5. **Testing Fixtures and Utilities**

**Pytest Fixtures** (examples from codebase):
- `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` uses `@pytest.fixture` decorator
- Fixtures can be app-level, client-level, or custom dependencies
- `monkeypatch` fixture used for mocking (e.g., `/tests/test_multipart_installation.py`)
- `tmp_path` fixture used for file operations (e.g., `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py`)

**Parametrization** (widely used):
```python
@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "path,expected_status,expected_response",
    [
        ("/api_route", 200, {"message": "Hello World"}),
        ("/nonexistent", 404, {"detail": "Not Found"}),
    ],
)
def test_get_path(path, expected_status, expected_response):
    response = client.get(path)
    assert response.status_code == expected_status
    assert response.json() == expected_response
```

### 6. **Test Client Features Used**

**Common Methods**:
- `client.get(path)` - GET request
- `client.post(path, json=...)` - POST with JSON body
- `client.request(method, path, ...)` - Custom HTTP method
- `client.head(path)` - HEAD request
- `client.options(path)` - OPTIONS request
- `client.patch(path, ...)` - PATCH request
- `client.delete(path, ...)` - DELETE request
- `client.websocket_connect(path)` - WebSocket connection
- `client.get(path, params=...)` - Query parameters
- `client.get(path, follow_redirects=False)` - Control redirects

**Context Manager Usage**:
```python
with client:
    response = client.get("/path")

# Also used with raise_server_exceptions parameter:
with TestClient(app, raise_server_exceptions=False) as client:
    response = client.get("/path")
```

**TestClient Parameters**:
- `raise_server_exceptions` (default True) - Whether to raise exceptions during request handling
- `base_url` - Base URL for requests (e.g., "https://testserver")

### 7. **Test Dependencies and Setup State**

**Global State Management** (from `/tests/test_dependency_contextmanager.py`):
- Module-level state dictionaries for tracking dependency execution
- State reset between test functions
- Used to verify dependency lifecycle (before yield, after yield)

**In-Test App Creation**:
```python
def test_multiple_path():
    app = FastAPI()
    
    @app.get("/test1")
    async def test(var: Annotated[str, Query()] = "bar"):
        return {"foo": var}
    
    client = TestClient(app)
    response = client.get("/test1")
```

### 8. **Assertion and Validation Patterns**

**Standard Response Validation**:
```python
response = client.get("/path")
assert response.status_code == 200, response.text
assert response.json() == expected_value
assert response.headers["content-type"] == "application/json"
```

**Error Handling with pytest**:
```python
with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
    client.get("/server-error")

with pytest.raises(FastAPIError, match='error message pattern'):
    # code that should raise
```

**Inline Snapshots** (using `inline_snapshot` library):
```python
from inline_snapshot import snapshot

response = client.get("/openapi.json")
assert response.json() == snapshot({...})
```

### 9. **Dependency Testing Patterns**

**Fixture Dependencies**:
```python
async def dep_counter():
    counter_holder["counter"] += 1
    return counter_holder["counter"]

@app.get("/counter/")
async def get_counter(count: int = Depends(dep_counter)):
    return {"counter": count}

# Test verifies caching and scope behavior
```

**Generator/Context Manager Dependencies**:
```python
async def asyncgen_state(state: dict[str, str] = Depends(get_state)):
    state["/async"] = "asyncgen started"
    yield state["/async"]
    state["/async"] = "asyncgen completed"

# Tests verify both before-yield and after-yield execution
```

### 10. **Test Dependencies (from pyproject.toml lines 165-188)**

Required packages:
- `pytest >=9.0.0` - Test framework
- `httpx >=0.23.0,<1.0.0` - HTTP client (via docs-tests group)
- `dirty-equals >=0.9.0` - Flexible assertion matching
- `inline-snapshot >=0.21.1` - Snapshot testing
- `pytest-xdist[psutil]>=2.5.0` - Parallel test execution
- `pytest-cov>=4.0.0` - Coverage reporting
- `pytest-sugar>=1.0.0` - Enhanced test output
- `pytest-timeout>=2.4.0` - Test timeouts
- `anyio[trio] >=3.2.1,<5.0.0` - Async testing support
- Additional tools: `mypy`, `flask`, `sqlmodel`, `strawberry-graphql`, etc.

### 11. **Coverage Configuration** (from pyproject.toml lines 241-262)

```
[tool.coverage.run]
parallel = true
data_file = "coverage/.coverage"
source = [
    "docs_src",
    "tests",
    "fastapi"
]
```

### 12. **File Patterns and Organization**

**Test File Examples Read**:
- `/tests/test_application.py` - 58KB+ file testing various application features
- `/tests/test_custom_middleware_exception.py` - 2.8KB middleware exception testing
- `/tests/test_dependency_contextmanager.py` - 14KB context manager/generator dependency testing
- `/tests/test_dependency_cache.py` - 3.6KB dependency caching behavior
- `/tests/test_annotated.py` - 10.7KB Annotated type testing
- `/tests/test_extra_routes.py` - 12KB HTTP method testing
- `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` - 3.7KB middleware with fixtures pattern
- `/tests/test_multipart_installation.py` - 5KB monkeypatch usage
- `/tests/test_http_connection_injection.py` - 1.3KB dependency injection testing

### 13. **No Test Configuration File**
- **No conftest.py** exists in the tests directory
- **No pytest.ini** file
- **No setup.cfg** with pytest configuration
- All configuration is in `pyproject.toml`
- Tests are self-contained with inline app and client creation

This is a comprehensive, pytest-based testing infrastructure with:
- Self-contained test files
- Heavy use of parametrization
- Both fixture-based and inline patterns
- Comprehensive middleware testing
- Dependency lifecycle verification
- Snapshot testing for complex schemas
- No centralized fixtures file

Perfect! Now I have all the key information. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here's what I found:

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW

**Project**: FastAPI Framework  
**Version**: 0.135.3  
**Framework**: Built on top of Starlette (>=0.46.0)  
**Python**: Requires Python 3.10+  
**Key Dependencies**:
- Starlette >= 0.46.0
- Pydantic >= 2.9.0
- typing-extensions >= 4.8.0

### 2. OVERALL PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
/fastapi/                              # Main FastAPI package
  ├── __init__.py                      # Exports FastAPI, Request, Response, APIRouter, etc.
  ├── applications.py                  # Core FastAPI class (182KB - largest file)
  ├── routing.py                       # Route handling and APIRouter (197KB)
  ├── middleware/                      # Middleware package
  ├── dependencies/                    # Dependency injection system
  ├── security/                        # Security utilities
  ├── openapi/                         # OpenAPI schema generation
  ├── params.py                        # Parameter definitions
  ├── responses.py                     # Response classes
  ├── exceptions.py                    # Custom exceptions
  └── [other files: encoders, sse, background, etc.]

/tests/                                # Test suite
  ├── test_rate_limiter.py            # NEW: Rate limiter tests
  ├── test_tutorial/                  # Tutorial tests
  └── test_request_params/            # Request parameter tests

/docs_src/                             # Documentation examples
  ├── middleware/                      # Middleware examples
  └── [tutorial files organized by feature]

/pyproject.toml                        # Project configuration with PDM
```

### 3. MIDDLEWARE PACKAGE STRUCTURE

**Location**: `/fastapi/middleware/`

**Current Middleware Files**:
1. `__init__.py` - Exports `Middleware` from Starlette
2. `cors.py` - Re-exports Starlette's CORSMiddleware
3. `gzip.py` - Re-exports Starlette's GZipMiddleware
4. `httpsredirect.py` - Re-exports Starlette's HTTPSRedirectMiddleware
5. `trustedhost.py` - Re-exports Starlette's TrustedHostMiddleware
6. `wsgi.py` - Re-exports Starlette's WSGIMiddleware
7. `asyncexitstack.py` - **FastAPI-specific** custom middleware for resource cleanup

**Pattern**: Most middleware are re-exports from Starlette. Only `AsyncExitStackMiddleware` is FastAPI-specific (used to close files after requests).

**How Middleware is Exported**:
```python
# Example from cors.py
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```

### 4. EXISTING RATE LIMITING/THROTTLING CODE

**Status**: NO rate limiting middleware currently exists in FastAPI.

**Test File Found**: `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` - This is a test specification file that expects a `RateLimitMiddleware` to be implemented.

**What the Test Expects**:
```python
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware

app.add_middleware(
    RateLimitMiddleware,
    requests_per_minute=60,
    burst_size=5,
)
```

**Expected Features** (from test analysis):
- Per-IP rate limiting (tracks by client IP)
- Configurable `requests_per_minute` parameter
- Configurable `burst_size` for burst allowance
- Returns 429 (Too Many Requests) when limit exceeded
- Response includes JSON with:
  - `detail`: Error message "Rate limit exceeded"
  - `retry_after`: Seconds until client can retry
- Response headers:
  - `X-RateLimit-Limit`: Configured limit (e.g., "60")
  - `X-RateLimit-Remaining`: Requests remaining in current window
  - `X-RateLimit-Reset`: Unix timestamp of reset time
- Rate limit applies across all paths for same IP (shared per IP)

### 5. FASTAPI APP SETUP AND MIDDLEWARE REGISTRATION

**Main Class**: `FastAPI` (extends `Starlette`)  
**Location**: `/fastapi/applications.py` (starts at line 45)

**FastAPI Constructor Parameters** (relevant to middleware):
- `middleware: Sequence[Middleware] | None = None` - Initial middleware list
- `debug: bool = False` - Debug mode
- `exception_handlers: dict | None = None` - Exception handler mapping

**Initialization Flow**:
```python
class FastAPI(Starlette):
    def __init__(self, ...):
        # Store user-provided middleware
        self.user_middleware: list[Middleware] = (
            [] if middleware is None else list(middleware)
        )
        self.middleware_stack: ASGIApp | None = None
        self.setup()  # Sets up OpenAPI routes
```

**Middleware Stack Building** (lines 1022-1070):

The `build_middleware_stack()` method creates a layered middleware stack:

```
ServerErrorMiddleware (outermost)
  ↓
ExceptionMiddleware
  ↓
[User Middleware] (added via add_middleware())
  ↓
AsyncExitStackMiddleware (FastAPI-specific)
  ↓
Router (the actual app)
```

**Order Matters**: 
- ServerErrorMiddleware wraps everything (handles 500 errors)
- User middleware added via `add_middleware()` go in the middle
- AsyncExitStackMiddleware is inside user middleware to preserve contextvars
- Router is the innermost component

**How Middleware is Added**:
1. **During initialization**: Pass `middleware` parameter with list of `Middleware(Class, ...)` objects
2. **After initialization**: Call `app.add_middleware(MiddlewareClass, **kwargs)` (inherited from Starlette)

**Example Usage** (from test file):
```python
app = FastAPI()
app.add_middleware(
    RateLimitMiddleware,
    requests_per_minute=60,
    burst_size=5,
)
```

The `add_middleware()` method is inherited from Starlette and appends to `self.user_middleware`.

### 6. REQUEST OBJECT

**Location**: `/fastapi/requests.py`
**Implementation**: Re-exports from Starlette
```python
from starlette.requests import Request as Request
```

**Available in Middleware**: Accessed via `scope["client"]` tuple `(host, port)` for IP extraction.

### 7. KEY FILES TO UNDERSTAND MIDDLEWARE IMPLEMENTATION

1. **`/fastapi/applications.py`** (4750+ lines)
   - Lines 1022-1070: `build_middleware_stack()` - Shows how middleware is layered
   - Lines 61-1020: `__init__()` constructor

2. **`/fastapi/middleware/asyncexitstack.py`** (19 lines)
   - Simple example of a custom middleware class
   - Uses ASGI protocol: `async def __call__(self, scope, receive, send)`

3. **`/tests/test_rate_limiter.py`**
   - Complete specification of expected RateLimitMiddleware behavior
   - 115 lines with 8 test methods

### 8. STARLETTE INTEGRATION POINTS

- FastAPI inherits from `Starlette` (starlette.applications)
- Middleware system uses Starlette's `Middleware` class for configuration
- Middlewares use ASGI protocol with `scope`, `receive`, `send` parameters
- Most built-in middleware is delegated to Starlette, reexported through FastAPI

---

**Summary**: The FastAPI codebase is well-structured with middleware being a key architectural component inherited from Starlette. The test file indicates that a `RateLimitMiddleware` needs to be implemented in `/fastapi/middleware/rate_limit.py`, following the same pattern as existing Starlette-based middleware. The middleware should track requests per IP, enforce burst limits and sustained rate limits, and return appropriate 429 responses with rate limit headers and retry information.

Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the findings:

## Summary of Codebase Exploration

Based on my thorough exploration of the FastAPI codebase, here are my findings:

### 1. Configuration Handling

**Configuration Files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775503495790-uo3zk5/pyproject.toml` - Main project configuration with dependencies, build system (pdm-backend), and tool configurations
- No `.env` or environment-specific config files found in the main codebase
- Configuration is primarily handled through the FastAPI application initialization

**FastAPI Application Initialization:**
- `FastAPI.__init__()` in `/fastapi/applications.py` (lines 61-1020) accepts extensive configuration parameters
- Key config parameters include:
  - `debug` - Boolean for debug mode (default: False)
  - `title`, `summary`, `description`, `version` - API metadata
  - `openapi_url`, `docs_url`, `redoc_url` - Documentation endpoints
  - `root_path` - Path prefix for proxy setups
  - `strict_content_type` - Controls Content-Type validation (default: True)
  - `middleware` - List of Middleware to add during initialization
  - `exception_handlers` - Dictionary for custom error handlers
  - `default_response_class` - Default response class (default: JSONResponse from Starlette)

**Configuration via `pydantic-settings`:**
- Listed as optional dependency in `pyproject.toml` (lines 73, 113)
- Not integrated into FastAPI core, but available for user applications

### 2. Dependencies Installed

**Core Dependencies (from pyproject.toml lines 44-50):**
- `starlette>=0.46.0` - ASGI framework foundation
- `pydantic>=2.9.0` - Data validation
- `typing-extensions>=4.8.0` - Type hints
- `typing-inspection>=0.4.2` - Type inspection utilities
- `annotated-doc>=0.0.2` - Documentation annotations

**Standard Optional Dependencies (lines 60-76):**
- `fastapi-cli>=0.0.8`
- `httpx>=0.23.0,<1.0.0` - HTTP client for testing
- `jinja2>=3.1.5` - Template engine
- `python-multipart>=0.0.18` - Form/file uploads
- `email-validator>=2.0.0` - Email validation
- `uvicorn[standard]>=0.12.0` - ASGI server
- `pydantic-settings>=2.0.0` - Settings management
- `pydantic-extra-types>=2.0.0` - Additional Pydantic types

**Testing Dependencies (lines 165-188):**
- `pytest>=9.0.0`, `pytest-cov`, `pytest-xdist`, `pytest-timeout`, etc.
- `httpx` for test client
- `anyio[trio]>=3.2.1,<5.0.0` - Async testing
- Additional tools: `mypy`, `coverage`, `ujson`, `orjson`, etc.

### 3. Error Response Patterns

**JSON Error Response Structure:**
From `/fastapi/exception_handlers.py`:
- **HTTPException handler** (lines 11-17):
  ```json
  {
    "detail": <exception.detail>,
    "status_code": <exception.status_code>,
    "headers": <exception.headers (optional)>
  }
  ```
  - Returns with appropriate `status_code`
  - Includes `detail` field with error information
  - Supports custom headers

- **RequestValidationError handler** (lines 20-26):
  ```json
  {
    "detail": [<array of validation errors>]
  }
  ```
  - Status code: 422 (Unprocessable Entity)
  - Returns array of Pydantic validation error objects

- **WebSocketRequestValidationError handler** (lines 29-34):
  - Closes WebSocket with code `WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION`
  - Includes error reasons in the close message

**Error Response for Status Codes Without Body:**
From `fastapi/utils.py`:
- Status codes 204, 304, and others in the "no-body" range return `Response(status_code=..., headers=...)` without JSON body

### 4. Response Header Patterns

**Standard Response Headers Used:**

From `/fastapi/routing.py` and `/fastapi/applications.py`:
- `Cache-Control: no-cache` (line 616 in routing.py) - For streaming responses
- `X-Accel-Buffering: no` (line 618 in routing.py) - For streaming responses (Nginx buffering control)
- `X-Process-Time` (line 4693 in applications.py) - Example custom header for timing middleware
- `X-Cat-Dog` (line 4076 in routing.py) - Example custom header

**Headers Access Pattern:**
- `response.headers["Header-Name"] = "value"` - Dictionary-like interface
- `response.headers.raw` - Raw header tuples for batch operations
- `response.headers.get("header-name")` - Case-insensitive retrieval

**From Exception Handlers:**
From `/fastapi/exceptions.py` (lines 70-81):
- `HTTPException` accepts optional `headers` parameter (dict)
- Headers are passed through to the response when exception is handled

**From Test Examples** (`/fastapi/tests/test_starlette_exception.py`):
- Custom headers via HTTPException: `headers={"X-Error": "Some custom header"}`
- Retrieved as: `response.headers.get("x-error")` (case-insensitive)

### 5. Middleware Package __init__.py

**File Location:** `/fastapi/middleware/__init__.py`

**Complete Content:**
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

**What it exports:**
- Re-exports Starlette's `Middleware` class directly as a named export
- This is the only thing in the FastAPI middleware package's `__init__.py`
- Actual middleware implementations are in separate modules:
  - `asyncexitstack.py` - AsyncExitStackMiddleware for resource cleanup
  - `cors.py` - CORS middleware wrapper
  - `gzip.py` - Gzip middleware wrapper
  - `httpsredirect.py` - HTTPS redirect middleware wrapper
  - `trustedhost.py` - Trusted host middleware wrapper
  - `wsgi.py` - WSGI middleware wrapper

**Usage Pattern:**
```python
from fastapi.middleware import Middleware
# or
from starlette.middleware import Middleware
```

**Middleware Stack Construction:**
From `/fastapi/applications.py` (lines 1022-1070):
- `build_middleware_stack()` method constructs ASGI middleware stack
- Order: ServerErrorMiddleware → User Middleware → ExceptionMiddleware → AsyncExitStackMiddleware
- Custom user middlewares wrap around the exception handling middleware

### Key Architectural Patterns

1. **Configuration is Initialization-Time:** FastAPI uses constructor parameters rather than environment variables or config files (though pydantic-settings is available)

2. **Response Headers are Mutable:** Headers can be set as dict items on Response objects: `response.headers["X-Custom"] = "value"`

3. **Error Responses Follow JSON Convention:** All error responses use JSON with a "detail" field containing error information

4. **Headers are Case-Insensitive:** HTTP headers follow RFC standards with case-insensitive names

5. **Middleware Wrapping:** User middlewares wrap exception handling, allowing them to catch and handle exceptions from dependencies

import time
import pytest
from fastapi import FastAPI
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware


@pytest.fixture
def app():
    app = FastAPI()
    app.add_middleware(
        RateLimitMiddleware,
        requests_per_minute=60,
        burst_size=5,
    )

    @app.get("/")
    async def root():
        return {"message": "hello"}

    @app.get("/other")
    async def other():
        return {"message": "other"}

    return app


@pytest.fixture
def client(app):
    return TestClient(app)


class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
        for _ in range(5):
            response = client.get("/")
            assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
        # Use up the burst
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        # Next request should be rate limited
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
        assert "retry_after" in body

    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
        response = client.get("/")
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers

    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
        r1 = client.get("/")
        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        r2 = client.get("/")
        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
        assert remaining2 < remaining1

    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 200
        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"

    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
        # Use up burst across different endpoints
        for _ in range(3):
            client.get("/")
        for _ in range(2):
            client.get("/other")
        # Should be rate limited now
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429

    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
        for _ in range(5):
            client.get("/")
        response = client.get("/")
        assert response.status_code == 429
        body = response.json()
        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
        assert body["retry_after"] > 0

    def test_custom_limits(self):
        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
        app = FastAPI()
        app.add_middleware(
            RateLimitMiddleware,
            requests_per_minute=120,
            burst_size=2,
        )

        @app.get("/")
        async def root():
            return {"ok": True}

        client = TestClient(app)
        # Burst of 2
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429

file.content:
from contextlib import AsyncExitStack

from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send


# Used mainly to close files after the request is done, dependencies are closed
# in their own AsyncExitStack
class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(
        self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack"
    ) -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)

1	from fastapi.encoders import jsonable_encoder
2	from fastapi.exceptions import RequestValidationError, WebSocketRequestValidationError
3	from fastapi.utils import is_body_allowed_for_status_code
4	from fastapi.websockets import WebSocket
5	from starlette.exceptions import HTTPException
6	from starlette.requests import Request
7	from starlette.responses import JSONResponse, Response
8	from starlette.status import WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION
9	
10	
11	async def http_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: HTTPException) -> Response:
12	    headers = getattr(exc, "headers", None)
13	    if not is_body_allowed_for_status_code(exc.status_code):
14	        return Response(status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers)
15	    return JSONResponse(
16	        {"detail": exc.detail}, status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers
17	    )
18	
19	
20	async def request_validation_exception_handler(
21	    request: Request, exc: RequestValidationError
22	) -> JSONResponse:
23	    return JSONResponse(
24	        status_code=422,
25	        content={"detail": jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())},
26	    )
27	
28	
29	async def websocket_request_validation_exception_handler(
30	    websocket: WebSocket, exc: WebSocketRequestValidationError
31	) -> None:
32	    await websocket.close(
33	        code=WS_1008_POLICY_VIOLATION, reason=jsonable_encoder(exc.errors())
34	    )

35	class UJSONResponse(JSONResponse):

48	    separately, e.g. `pip install ujson`.

52	        assert ujson is not None, "ujson must be installed to use UJSONResponse"

from typing import Any

from fastapi.exceptions import FastAPIDeprecationWarning
from fastapi.sse import EventSourceResponse as EventSourceResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import FileResponse as FileResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import HTMLResponse as HTMLResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import JSONResponse as JSONResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import PlainTextResponse as PlainTextResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import RedirectResponse as RedirectResponse  # noqa
from starlette.responses import Response as Response  # noqa
from starlette.responses import StreamingResponse as StreamingResponse  # noqa
from typing_extensions import deprecated

try:
    import ujson
except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
    ujson = None  # type: ignore


try:
    import orjson
except ImportError:  # pragma: nocover
    orjson = None  # type: ignore


@deprecated(
    "UJSONResponse is deprecated, FastAPI now serializes data directly to JSON "
    "bytes via Pydantic when a return type or response model is set, which is "
    "faster and doesn't need a custom response class. Read more in the FastAPI "
    "docs: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/custom-response/#orjson-or-response-model "
    "and https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/response-model/",
    category=FastAPIDeprecationWarning,
    stacklevel=2,
)
class UJSONResponse(JSONResponse):
    """JSON response using the ujson library to serialize data to JSON.

    **Deprecated**: `UJSONResponse` is deprecated. FastAPI now serializes data
    directly to JSON bytes via Pydantic when a return type or response model is
    set, which is faster and doesn't need a custom response class.

    Read more in the
    [FastAPI docs for Custom Response](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/custom-response/#orjson-or-response-model)
    and the
    [FastAPI docs for Response Model](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/response-model/).

    **Note**: `ujson` is not included with FastAPI and must be installed
    separately, e.g. `pip install ujson`.
    """

    def render(self, content: Any) -> bytes:
        assert ujson is not None, "ujson must be installed to use UJSONResponse"
        return ujson.dumps(content, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")


@deprecated(
    "ORJSONResponse is deprecated, FastAPI now serializes data directly to JSON "
    "bytes via Pydantic when a return type or response model is set, which is "
    "faster and doesn't need a custom response class. Read more in the FastAPI "
    "docs: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/custom-response/#orjson-or-response-model "
    "and https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/response-model/",
    category=FastAPIDeprecationWarning,
    stacklevel=2,
)
class ORJSONResponse(JSONResponse):
    """JSON response using the orjson library to serialize data to JSON.

    **Deprecated**: `ORJSONResponse` is deprecated. FastAPI now serializes data
    directly to JSON bytes via Pydantic when a return type or response model is
    set, which is faster and doesn't need a custom response class.

    Read more in the
    [FastAPI docs for Custom Response](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/advanced/custom-response/#orjson-or-response-model)
    and the
    [FastAPI docs for Response Model](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/response-model/).

    **Note**: `orjson` is not included with FastAPI and must be installed
    separately, e.g. `pip install orjson`.
    """

    def render(self, content: Any) -> bytes:
        assert orjson is not None, "orjson must be installed to use ORJSONResponse"
        return orjson.dumps(
            content, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS | orjson.OPT_SERIALIZE_NUMPY
        )

I need to design a rate limiting middleware for a FastAPI project. Here's the full context:

## What exists already

### Project: FastAPI framework itself (version 0.135.3)
- Built on Starlette >= 0.46.0, Python 3.10+
- Middleware package at `/fastapi/middleware/`

### Existing middleware pattern (from `asyncexitstack.py` - the only custom FastAPI middleware):
```python
from starlette.types import ASGIApp, Receive, Scope, Send

class AsyncExitStackMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, app: ASGIApp, context_name: str = "fastapi_middleware_astack") -> None:
        self.app = app
        self.context_name = context_name

    async def __call__(self, scope: Scope, receive: Receive, send: Send) -> None:
        async with AsyncExitStack() as stack:
            scope[self.context_name] = stack
            await self.app(scope, receive, send)
```

### Other middleware files are just re-exports:
```python
# cors.py
from starlette.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware as CORSMiddleware  # noqa
```

### Middleware __init__.py only exports:
```python
from starlette.middleware import Middleware as Middleware
```

### Test file already exists at `/tests/test_rate_limiter.py` with these exact expectations:

1. **Import**: `from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware`
2. **Usage**: `app.add_middleware(RateLimitMiddleware, requests_per_minute=60, burst_size=5)`
3. **Tests**:
   - `test_allows_requests_under_limit` - 5 requests succeed when burst_size=5
   - `test_blocks_requests_over_burst` - 6th request returns 429 with `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <float>}`
   - `test_rate_limit_headers_present` - All responses have X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
   - `test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases` - Remaining header decreases between requests
   - `test_rate_limit_per_ip` - X-RateLimit-Limit header equals "60"
   - `test_different_paths_share_limit` - Rate limit is shared across endpoints for same IP
   - `test_429_response_format` - 429 body has `detail` (str) and `retry_after` (int/float > 0)
   - `test_custom_limits` - Different app with requests_per_minute=120, burst_size=2; 3rd request gets 429

### Error response pattern from FastAPI:
```python
JSONResponse({"detail": exc.detail}, status_code=exc.status_code, headers=headers)
```

## Requirements
- Token bucket algorithm, per-client IP
- Configurable: `requests_per_minute`, `burst_size`
- 429 response with JSON `{"detail": "Rate limit exceeded", "retry_after": <seconds>}`
- X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset headers on ALL responses (200 and 429)
- ASGI middleware pattern (not Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware)
- No external dependencies

## Design questions to address:
1. Token bucket implementation details - how to track tokens per IP, refill rate
2. How to intercept ASGI `send` to inject headers into successful responses
3. How to handle non-HTTP scopes (websocket, lifespan)
4. Thread safety for the token bucket state (asyncio is single-threaded but still need to be careful)
5. How to extract client IP from ASGI scope
6. Memory management - should we clean up old IP entries?

Please provide a detailed implementation plan with code structure and algorithm details.

3	from fastapi import FastAPI

5	from fastapi.middleware.rate_limit import RateLimitMiddleware
6

8	@pytest.fixture
9	def app():
10	    app = FastAPI()
11	    app.add_middleware(
12	        RateLimitMiddleware,
13	        requests_per_minute=60,
14	        burst_size=5,
15	    )
16	
17	    @app.get("/")
18	    async def root():
19	        return {"message": "hello"}
20	
21	    @app.get("/other")
22	    async def other():
23	        return {"message": "other"}
24	
25	    return app
26

28	@pytest.fixture
29	def client(app):
30	    return TestClient(app)
31	
32	
33	class TestRateLimitMiddleware:
34	    def test_allows_requests_under_limit(self, client):
35	        """Requests under the burst size should succeed."""
36	        for _ in range(5):
37	            response = client.get("/")
38	            assert response.status_code == 200
39	
40	    def test_blocks_requests_over_burst(self, client):
41	        """Requests exceeding burst size should be blocked."""
42	        # Use up the burst
43	        for _ in range(5):
44	            client.get("/")
45	        # Next request should be rate limited
46	        response = client.get("/")
47	        assert response.status_code == 429
48	        body = response.json()
49	        assert "Rate limit exceeded" in body["detail"]
50	        assert "retry_after" in body
51	
52	    def test_rate_limit_headers_present(self, client):
53	        """All responses should include rate limit headers."""
54	        response = client.get("/")
55	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
56	        assert "X-RateLimit-Remaining" in response.headers
57	        assert "X-RateLimit-Reset" in response.headers
58	
59	    def test_rate_limit_remaining_decreases(self, client):
60	        """Remaining count should decrease with each request."""
61	        r1 = client.get("/")
62	        remaining1 = int(r1.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
63	        r2 = client.get("/")
64	        remaining2 = int(r2.headers["X-RateLimit-Remaining"])
65	        assert remaining2 < remaining1
66	
67	    def test_rate_limit_per_ip(self, client):
68	        """Rate limits should be per-client IP."""
69	        # TestClient always uses the same IP, so this tests the basic flow
70	        response = client.get("/")
71	        assert response.status_code == 200
72	        assert "X-RateLimit-Limit" in response.headers
73	        assert response.headers["X-RateLimit-Limit"] == "60"
74	
75	    def test_different_paths_share_limit(self, client):
76	        """Rate limit applies across all paths for the same client."""
77	        # Use up burst across different endpoints
78	        for _ in range(3):
79	            client.get("/")
80	        for _ in range(2):
81	            client.get("/other")
82	        # Should be rate limited now
83	        response = client.get("/")
84	        assert response.status_code == 429
85	
86	    def test_429_response_format(self, client):
87	        """429 response should have proper JSON format."""
88	        for _ in range(5):
89	            client.get("/")
90	        response = client.get("/")
91	        assert response.status_code == 429
92	        body = response.json()
93	        assert isinstance(body["detail"], str)
94	        assert isinstance(body["retry_after"], (int, float))
95	        assert body["retry_after"] > 0
96	
97	    def test_custom_limits(self):
98	        """Should respect custom rate limit configuration."""
99	        app = FastAPI()
100	        app.add_middleware(
101	            RateLimitMiddleware,
102	            requests_per_minute=120,
103	            burst_size=2,
104	        )
105	
106	        @app.get("/")
107	        async def root():
108	            return {"ok": True}
109	
110	        client = TestClient(app)
111	        # Burst of 2
112	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
113	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 200
114	        assert client.get("/").status_code == 429
